title: Real sub-agent tooling — replace NoopToolExecutor and Rhai stubs
purpose: SpawnAgent and Fork currently give sub-agents a NoopToolExecutor, meaning spawned/forked agents can only do text completion — any tool call returns ToolNotFound. The Rhai spawn_agent and fork_agent builtins return canned stub payloads instead of actually running agents. This brief makes multi-agent work real: sub-agents get a real ToolRegistry, and the Rhai bridge actually calls run_agent_step.
task: Add tool_registry field to AgentToolInfra. SpawnAgentTool clones the parent registry, applies set_available for requested tool subset, passes as ToolExecutor. ForkTool passes registry to fork(). Remove both NoopToolExecutor definitions. Rhai spawn_agent uses runtime.spawn to run run_agent_step asynchronously, stores JoinHandle in SpawnedAgent. Rhai wait_agent awaits the JoinHandle via block_in_place. Rhai fork_agent calls the real fork() function via block_in_place. Testing: MockProvider tests verifying sub-agents execute tool calls.

requirements.title: Add ToolRegistry to AgentToolInfra
requirements.spec: AgentToolInfra SHALL carry an optional ToolRegistry that sub-agents receive. When present, spawned and forked agents get a real tool executor instead of NoopToolExecutor.
acceptance: AgentToolInfra gains field: tool_registry (Option<Arc<ToolRegistry>>)
When tool_registry is Some, SpawnAgentTool clones it for the sub-agent
When tool_registry is None, SpawnAgentTool returns an error explaining no tools available
Remove NoopToolExecutor from tools/agent/infra.rs
Remove NoopToolExecutor from agent/fork.rs
Test: construct AgentToolInfra with a registry containing a mock tool, spawn sub-agent, verify tool is callable

requirements.title: SpawnAgent uses real ToolRegistry
requirements.spec: SpawnAgentTool SHALL clone the parent's ToolRegistry for the sub-agent. If the spawn request specifies a tool subset, set_available restricts the clone to those tools.
acceptance: SpawnAgentTool::execute clones Arc<ToolRegistry> from AgentToolInfra
If args.tools is Some(names), calls set_available(names) on the clone
Passes the clone as &dyn ToolExecutor to run_agent_step
Sub-agent can successfully execute tools from the parent's registry
Test: spawn with tools=['search'], verify search tool works but bash returns ToolNotFound

requirements.title: Fork uses real ToolRegistry
requirements.spec: The fork() function SHALL accept an optional ToolExecutor parameter instead of using NoopToolExecutor. ForkTool passes the registry from AgentToolInfra.
acceptance: fork() signature gains: executor (Arc<dyn ToolExecutor>)
ForkTool::execute passes infra.tool_registry or a default empty registry
ForkConfig.tools used to restrict available tools via set_available
Test: fork with a tool-using prompt, verify tool executes in the forked context

requirements.title: Rhai spawn_agent — real implementation
requirements.spec: The Rhai spawn_agent builtin SHALL actually call run_agent_step via tokio::task::block_in_place, replacing the current stub that returns canned JSON.
acceptance: SpawnedAgent struct holds JoinHandle<Result<AgentStepResult, NornError>> instead of Option<Value>
spawn_agent registers in AgentRegistry, spawns run_agent_step via runtime.spawn()
Provider and ToolRegistry obtained from NornRhaiContext
Sub-agent receives the real ToolRegistry (same as R1-R2 pattern)
Comment 'spawn currently produces a deterministic stub payload' is removed
Test: Rhai script calls spawn_agent, wait_agent returns actual model output

requirements.title: Rhai fork_agent — real implementation
requirements.spec: The Rhai fork_agent builtin SHALL call the real fork() function from agent/fork.rs via block_in_place, replacing the current stub.
acceptance: fork_agent calls crate::agent::fork::fork() with real provider and registry
Uses tokio::task::block_in_place for async-sync bridge
Returns the ForkAudit result as a Rhai Dynamic value (serde conversion)
Stub 'status: forked' payload removed
Test: Rhai script calls fork_agent, verify ForkAudit fields present in result

requirements.title: Rhai wait_agent — real JoinHandle await
requirements.spec: wait_agent SHALL await the JoinHandle from spawn_agent instead of returning a stored stub value.
acceptance: wait_agent uses block_in_place to await the JoinHandle
Returns the AgentStepResult serialized as Rhai Dynamic
Timeout support: if the agent hasn't completed within a configurable duration, return an error
If the JoinHandle panicked, return a clear error (not a panic propagation)
Test: spawn_agent then wait_agent, verify result matches expected model output

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the ToolRegistry implementation — only consume it
SHALL NOT modify run_agent_step signature — use the existing (or N-017's LoopContext) API
SHALL NOT implement agent-to-agent tool delegation (agent A using agent B's tools) — sub-agents get a copy of the parent's registry
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all tests pass
grep -rn 'deterministic stub\|stub payload\|status.*forked' crates/norn/src/integration/rhai.rs returns no matches
A spawned sub-agent successfully executes a tool call — verified by test
A Rhai spawn_agent + wait_agent round-trip returns real model output — verified by test
